observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing, we recommended the following For RPT, conduct more extensive tax campaign so that landowners are apprised of the advantages/services that the Municipality would render if taxes are paid. Develop an efficient and effective monitoring and collection system so as to intensify the collection of its outstanding RPT/SET Receivables. Moreover, avail of all the possible remedies provided under the Rules and Regulations Implementing the Local Government Code of 1991 to enforce collection of RPT.

For Business Tax, enforce the right of the LGU to close the business if owners persist to operate despite the non-payment of the required permits, fees and taxes. In addition, consider updating the revenue code (business tax) in order to reflect the current rates based on inflation.

For Waterworks System Fees, enforce disconnection of service lines in order to impose payment. Moreover, provide a penalty provision in the revenue code to encourage early payment and discourage, if not deter, late payments.

We recommended that the BAC strictly comply with the procedures on receipts, opening and preliminary examination of bids as provided for in the Procurement Manual for LGUs and the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

However, we recommended that the BAC comply to the posting requirements in its Agency Website of the Notices of Award, Notices to Proceed and Approved Contracts for the procurement of goods, services and infrastructure projects through public bidding to promote transparency and efficiency in the conduct of procurement.

We recommended that management charge the financial assistance to general fund rather than to the GAD fund. Lastly, refrain to allocate a specific budget from the GAD fund under the offices of the elected officials for payment of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly utilize the 20% Development Fund to only eligible capital expenditures and ensure that the identified programs/projects are those mentioned under DILG-DBM Joint  Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 and Section 287 of RA 7160.

We recommended that full implementation of these programmed projects be fast tracked/prioritized and maximized.  Ensure that all the identified priority programs/projects/activities in the Annual Investment Plan be implemented as programmed to enjoy its timely benefit.

We recommended that management:
a]	strictly observe the provisions of Section 272 of the Local Government Code, Article 363 of its IRR and DECS, DBM and DILG Joint No. 01, s. of 1998 in utilizing Special Education Fund hence, utilize fully the SEF for its intended purpose;
b] 	if necessary, cash advances for Dep-ed activities drawn from LGU funds be made by the Municipal Treasurer or designate a Special Disbursing Officer for this matter and liquidate as soon as the purpose for which it was given is served; and
c]	require the Local School Board (LSB) to prepare a balanced budget for the SEF giving priority to the much needed classroom improvements and learning facilities for a better quality of K1 to 12 program of the Dep-Ed.
We recommended that management give priority in establishing VAW Desk in every barangay to address concerns of violence against women.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC Municipal Treasurer conduct regular reconciliation of the cash book and SL/GL    balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions   and the balance of Cash in Local Treasury account as of a given period.

We recommended that the management request authority from the local sanggunian to facilitate the transfer of funds maintained with DBP to an authorized government depository bank to avoid wastage of government resources.

We recommended management to:
a] 	Require the Municipal Accountant to set up RPT/SET Receivables based on the RPTAR maintained by the Municipal Assessor;
b] 	Require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish a copy of duly certified list of taxpayers including the amount due and collectible during the year to the Municipal Accountant as his basis in reconciling his RPT/SET Receivables accounts; and
c] 	Conduct regular reconciliation of balances, and if there’s any discrepancy, effect adjustment.

We recommended that the GSO and the Accounting Office conduct regular reconciliation of inventory accounts.  Trace discrepancies and make proper adjustments.  Also, require the GSO to update SCs for each type of supplies and materials while the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain SLCs for supplies and materials acquired and issued to support the year-end balances of the supplies and materials inventory and expense accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office record all  purchases to its corresponding inventory account classification and to adjust this account for whatever consumption on the basis of Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI), duly supported with the original copy of Requisition Issue Slip (RIS), which should be prepared by the requisitioning office to be submitted weekly or monthly to the Municipal Accountant pursuant to Section 121, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended that management:
a] 	prioritize the reconciliation of balances between the accounting records with the property inventory report.  Prepare adjusting journal entry/ies to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts; and
b] 	adopt a uniform valuation/recognition/measurement of properties in the property card and the accounting record.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant present all valid year-end payable accounts including its supporting documents to the Audit Team for verification and to prove that such payable accounts exist.
We recommended that the Accounting Office maintain a subsidiary ledger for Due to BIR and other related accounts to keep track balances and avoid existence of unaccounted balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for all the accounts appearing in the financial statements to determine at a given time the details and status of these accounts.  Apply the guidelines and procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on the dormant receivable accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office:
a] 	Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the  	necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all  	reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
b] 	Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error  	and omissions.

We recommended that the Accounting Office:
a] 	prepare necessary adjusting entry of the accounts affected for the  	fairness of accounts presentation in the financial statements; and
b] 	strictly monitor balances of accounts from time to time to avoid  	adjustments, if possible.

We recommended management to undertake effective, efficient and aggressive tax collection efforts and develop strategies to improve tax collection efficiency.  We further recommended that the concerned municipal officials to seek authority and assistance from the provincial government as well as the concerned barangays for the issuance of Notices of Delinquency and in the service of Warrants of Levy to delinquent real property owners.

We recommended that management require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to comply strictly with the fidelity bond requirement by applying additional bond for those accountable officers with insufficient amount of bond.   Also, for those who are not yet bonded, apply the correct amount of bond.  Furthermore, ensure that the amount of cash advances granted to the SDO are within the limits of his maximum cash accountability and the fidelity bond be renewed before expiry.  Monitor the validity period of approved applications for bond to ensure that all active accountable officers are properly bonded in accordance with law.
We recommended management to strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 1 and 3 of CSC Resolution No. 02-790 dated June 5, 2002.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain SL for each Accountable Officer for all their cash advances to facilitate monitoring of individual balances and accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a] 	Instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to maintain separate cash book to record his Cash Advances or Withdrawals per fund and per account in order to easily monitor his accountability and as basis in reconciliation against the subsidiary ledger, if there’s any.
b] 	Require the designated Special Disbursing Officers (SDO) to maintain cashbook for each fund/account.  Regular recording be made and balances be posted at the end of each month in order to determine the amount of her accountabilities for a certain period.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer  maintain separate logbook or folder for all the issuances of accountable forms to barangays.  Strict monitoring of remittances on CTC shall be imposed prior to issuance of another stub.  In like manner, for AF 51, require the concerned barangays to present or submit copy of validated deposit slip and duly verified collections reports prior to issuance of another stub.  This is one way of ensuring that these deputized collectors/barangay treasurers promptly remit or deposit all their collections.

We recommended that management instruct the accounting and treasury offices to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund and remit to the Bureau of Treasury the amount of ₱1,182,358.65 and ₱1,710.00, respectively.

We recommended that management:
a] 	adhere to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2013-004 and 2016-003 on the specifications for the signboards, tarpaulins to be posted where the projects/programs and activities of the local government unit in order to fully adopt the full disclosure policy governing public interest;
b] 	furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and
c] 	refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.
We recommended that management to properly attach all the necessary documents for all disbursements of infrastructure projects as required under COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for each special account in the general fund and prepare the corresponding financial reports to form part of the schedules of the general fund trial balance as required under the stated guidelines.  This will serve as a tool in informing management on the condition as well as the status of operation of these special accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a] require the persons liable to fully settle the NS and ND; and
b] 	ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the suspensions and disallowances for which they are liable, otherwise, the officials who issued their clearances shall be jointly liable for the unsettled disallowances of the retired employees.

We have recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF-MOOE for CYs 2011 to 2015 to Trust Fund books, then deposit the funds to a newly opened Special Trust bank account to be utilized exclusively to DRRM programs, projects and activities within the next five years.

observations.recommendation:
Cash advances were granted despite existence of huge unsettled balances.

Several cash advances were not immediately settled or reported after the purpose has been served.

Several cash advances were not supported with estimate of expenses.

Documents to support the liquidations of these cash advances were not separately submitted to the COA Office for audit and for issuance of Credit Notice to the Accountable Officer, pursuant to paragraph 5.4 of COA Circular No. 97-002, instead, some were included in the check disbursement vouchers, posting difficulty in tracing these liquidation documents.

We have recommended the following to the management:

Adhere strictly with the governing laws, rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances;

Send demand letters to all concerned officials and employees, active or retired from government service pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-004 and enforce settlement/s of their unsettled balances; 

Not issue clearances for money and property accountabilities to municipal officials and employees who are due to retire if they have outstanding balances.  Failure to do so will make the approving/signing authority primary or jointly liable for the amount given as provided under Sections 102 and 104 of P.D. 1445.
